name: D-ZINE Launch
description: A D-ZINE-specific launch profile for source-backed AI design systems, taste contracts, inspection surfaces, and open-source proof.
licenseNotes: Uses source-backed principles and D-ZINE-owned identity rules. Does not copy protected design systems, product surfaces, or agency case-study visuals.
principles: The source layer is the brand: show documents, paths, contracts, commands, audits, and critique artifacts.
Every claim should feel inspectable by an engineer and legible to a designer.
The website should behave like an operating manual with moments of editorial force.
Use proof rails and contract blocks instead of generic feature cards.
Taste is framed as provenance plus critique, not personal mystique.
positiveExamples: A hero that stages the generated source artifact and taste registry as product evidence.
A Taste Graph page that explains sources, contracts, community review, and guarded learning.
A docs route that teaches agents and humans where to look before implementing.
antiPatterns: Generic dark hero plus beige cards.
Rounded card grid as the only layout idea.
Terminal visuals used as fake developer credibility.
Taste claims with no source URLs or audit commands.
Brand identity that could belong to any AI tool.
typographyRules: Use display type for critique-grade statements, not every small panel.
Use body text for precise instructions and source-backed explanation.
Use mono text as evidence: source paths, registry keys, commands, and report locations.
layoutRules: Lead sections should combine claim, proof rail, and source artifact.
Alternate inspection panes, contract rails, critique comparisons, and docs panels.
Use fewer repeated cards; prefer named artifacts and process diagrams.
Mobile should feel like a designed document stack, not a compressed desktop.
componentRules: GitHub stars pill must remain a normal link with fallback text.
Taste profile cards must include status and provenance.
Proof cards must reference actual repo files, commands, routes, or reports.
copyRules: Say where taste comes from before claiming taste.
Use product-specific nouns: registry, profile, contract, critique, audit, source, route, screenshot.
Avoid broad supremacy claims unless tied to a visible proof artifact.
screenshotNotes: Screenshots should expose the source-to-output loop.
Review mobile screenshots for document-stack rhythm and not just pass/fail overflow.
